metadata.purpose: Knowledge-based Multiple Choice Questions for A Level Physics - Heatmap/Concept Understanding
metadata.type: Formula and concept recall (no direct working required)
metadata.structure: Grouped by topic, sorted by difficulty (1: Easy, 2: Medium, 3: Hard)

questions.question: Which quantity with its unit is correct?
options: electric current in a lamp = 0.25 Asâپ»آ¹
electric potential difference across a battery = 8.0 J Câپ»آ¹
kinetic energy of a car = 4500 Nmâپ»آ¹

questions.question: Which list contains only SI base units?
What is the unit of weight in terms of SI base unit(s)?
Which list shows increasing lengths from beginning to end?
Which equation contains only scalar quantities?
Which row gives reasonable estimates for the mass and the speed of an adult running?

When a force F moves its point of application through a displacement s in the direction of the force, the work W done by the force is given by W = Fs. How many vector quantities and scalar quantities does this equation contain?
options: one scalar quantity and two vector quantities
one vector quantity and two scalar quantities

questions.question: The density of the material of a coil of thin wire is to be found. Which set of instruments could be used to do this most accurately?
options: tape measure, vernier calipers, lever balance

questions.question: Which is an SI base unit?
Which pair contains one vector and one scalar quantity?
Which definition is correct and uses only quantities rather than units?
options: Density is mass per cubic metre.
Potential difference is energy per unit current.
Pressure is force per unit area.
Speed is distance travelled per second.

questions.question: What is the joule (J) in SI base units?
A student is given a reel of wire of diameter less than 0.2 mm and is asked to find the density of the metal. Which pair of instruments would be most suitable for finding the volume of the wire?
Which list contains one vector quantity and two scalar quantities?
Which pair of units contains one derived unit and one SI base unit?
What is equivalent to 2000 microvolts?
Which quantity can be measured in electronvolts (eV)?

The resistance of a lamp is calculated from the value of the potential difference (p.d.) across it and the value of the current passing through it. Which statement correctly describes how to combine the uncertainties in the p.d. and in the current?
options: Add together the actual uncertainty in the p.d. and the actual uncertainty in the current.
Add together the percentage uncertainty in the p.d. and the percentage uncertainty in the current.
Subtract the actual uncertainty in the current from the actual uncertainty in the p.d.
Subtract the percentage uncertainty in the current from the percentage uncertainty in the p.d.

questions.question: Which row shows an SI base quantity with its correct unit?

Two forces of equal magnitude are represented by two coplanar vectors. One is directed eastwards and the other is directed northwards. What is the direction of a single force that will balance these two forces?
Which pair of quantities contains one vector and one scalar quantity?
A wave has a frequency of 5 GHz. What is the period of the wave?
Which pair includes a vector quantity and a scalar quantity?
What is the unit of power, expressed in SI base units?

A student takes measurements of the current in a resistor of constant resistance and the potential difference (p.d.) across it. The readings are then used to plot a graph of current against p.d. There is a systematic error in the current readings. How could this be identified from the graph?
options: At least one anomalous data point can be identified.
The data points are scattered about the straight line of best fit.
The graph is a curve, not a straight line.
The straight line graph does not pass through the origin.

questions.question: What is the unit of weight in terms of SI base unit(s)?
Which list contains only scalar quantities?
The density of the material of a coil of thin wire is to be found. Which set of instruments could be used to do this most accurately?
options: tape measure, vernier calipers, lever balance

questions.question: The measurement of a physical quantity may be subject to random errors and to systematic errors. Which statement is correct?
options: Random errors can be reduced by taking the average of several measurements.
Random errors are always caused by the person taking the measurement.
A systematic error cannot be reduced by adjusting the apparatus.
A systematic error results in a different reading each time the measurement is taken.

questions.question: Physical quantities can be classed as vectors or as scalars. Which pair of quantities are both vectors?
A student is given a reel of wire of diameter less than 0.2 mm and is asked to find the density of the metal. Which pair of instruments would be most suitable for finding the volume of the wire?
What will reduce the systematic errors when taking a measurement?
options: adjusting the needle on a voltmeter so that it reads zero when there is no potential difference across it
measuring the diameter of a wire at different points and taking the average
reducing the parallax effects by using a marker and a mirror when measuring the amplitude of oscillation of a pendulum
timing 20 oscillations, rather than a single oscillation, when finding the period of a pendulum

questions.question: When a force F moves its point of application through a displacement s in the direction of the force, the work W done by the force is given by W = Fs. How many vector quantities and scalar quantities does this equation contain?
options: one scalar quantity and two vector quantities
one vector quantity and two scalar quantities

questions.question: What is the unit watt in terms of SI base units?
Which statement using prefixes of the base unit metre (m) is not correct?
Which group of quantities contains only vectors?
Which quantity can be measured in electronvolts (eV)?

The speedometer in a car consists of a pointer which rotates. The pointer is situated several millimetres from a calibrated scale. What could cause a random error in the driver's measurement of the car's speed?
options: The car's speed is affected by the wind direction.
The driver's eye is not always in the same position in relation to the pointer.
The speedometer does not read zero when the car is at rest.
The speedometer reads 10% higher than the car's actual speed.

questions.question: Decimal sub-multiples and multiples of units are indicated using a prefix to the unit. For example, the prefix milli (m) represents 10âپ»آ³. Which row gives the sub-multiples or multiples represented by pico (p) and giga (G)?
Which definition is correct and uses only quantities rather than units?
options: Density is mass per cubic metre.
Potential difference is energy per unit current.
Pressure is force per unit area.
Speed is distance travelled per second.

questions.question: Stress has the same SI base units as
A signal has a frequency of 2.0 MHz. What is the period of the signal?
Which physical quantity would result from a calculation in which a potential difference is multiplied by an electric charge?
Which row shows a base quantity with its correct SI unit?
A metre rule is used to measure the length of a piece of wire. It is found to be 70 cm long to the nearest millimetre. How should this result be recorded in a table of results?
Which pair of quantities do not have the same SI base units?
options: electromotive force and electric potential difference
spring constant and moment of a force

questions.question: The luminosity L of a star is given by L = 4د€rآ²دƒTâپ´ where r is the radius of the star, T is the temperature of the star, دƒ is a constant with units W mâپ»آ² Kâپ»âپ´. What are the SI base units of L?
In making reasonable estimates of physical quantities, which statement is not correct?
options: The frequency of sound can be of the order of GHz.
The wavelength of light can be of the order of 600 nm.
The Young modulus of a metal can be of the order of 10آ¹آ¹ Pa.
Beta particles are associated with one unit of negative charge.

questions.question: The time T taken for a satellite to orbit the Earth on a circular path is given by the equation Tآ² = krآ³ / M where r is the radius of the orbit, M is the mass of the Earth and k is a constant. What are the SI base units of k?
What is the unit of the Young modulus when expressed in SI base units?

The Reynolds number R is a constant used in the study of liquids flowing through pipes. R is a pure number with no unit. R = دپvD / آµ where دپ is the density of the liquid, v is the speed of the liquid and D is the diameter of the pipe through which the liquid flows. What are the SI base units of آµ?
Measurements are subject to systematic error and random error. Which measurements have high accuracy and low precision?
options: high random error and high systematic error
high random error and low systematic error
low random error and high systematic error
low random error and low systematic error

questions.question: A whale produces sound waves of frequency 5 Hz. The waves are detected by a microphone and displayed on an oscilloscope. What is the time-base setting on the oscilloscope?
The average kinetic energy E of a gas molecule is given by the equation E = 1.5kT where T is the absolute (kelvin) temperature. What are the SI base units of k?
Which statement includes a correct unit?

A 0.10 kg mass is taken to Mars and then weighed on a spring balance and on a lever balance. The acceleration due to gravity on Mars is 38% of its value on Earth. What are the readings on the two balances on Mars? (Assume that on Earth g = 10 msâپ»آ².)
options: spring balance = 0.38 N, lever balance = 0.038 kg
spring balance = 0.38 N, lever balance = 0.10 kg
spring balance = 1.0 N, lever balance = 0.038 kg
spring balance = 1.0 N, lever balance = 0.10 kg

questions.question: When the brakes are applied on a vehicle moving at speed v, the distance d moved by the vehicle in coming to rest is given by the expression d = kvآ² where k is a constant. What is the unit of k expressed in SI base units?

The speed v of a liquid leaving a tube depends on the change in pressure خ”P and the density دپ of the liquid. The speed is given by the equation v = k(خ”P / دپ)âپ؟ where k is a constant that has no units. What is the value of n?

The maximum theoretical power P of a wind turbine is given by the equation P = kدپAvâپ؟ where دپ is the density of air, A is the area swept by the turbine blades, v is the speed of the air and k is a constant with no units. What is the value of n?
What is the unit of resistance when expressed in SI base units?
A quantity y is to be determined from the equation y = px / qآ². The percentage uncertainties in p, x and q are 6%, 2% and 4% respectively. What is the percentage uncertainty in y?
The unit of specific heat capacity is J kgâپ»آ¹ Kâپ»آ¹. What is its equivalent in terms of SI base units?

The drag coefficient Cd is a number with no units. It is used to compare the drag on different cars at different speeds. It is given by the equation Cd = 2F / (دپvâپ؟A) where F is the drag force on the car, دپ is the density of the air, A is the cross-sectional area of the car and v is the speed of the car. What is the value of n?
Which estimate is realistic?
options: The kinetic energy of a bus travelling on an expressway is 30000 J.
The power of a domestic light is 300 W.
The temperature of a hot oven is 300 K.
The volume of air in a car tyre is 0.03 mآ³.

questions.question: Which unit is equivalent to the coulomb?

One property Q of a material is used to describe the behaviour of sound waves in the material. Q is defined as the pressure P of the sound wave divided by the speed v of the wave and the surface area A of the material through which the wave travels: Q = P / (vA). What are the SI base units of Q?

In an experiment to determine the acceleration of free fall g, the period of oscillation T and length l of a simple pendulum were measured. The uncertainty in the measurement of l is estimated to be 4%, and the uncertainty in the measurement of T is estimated to be 1%. The value of g is determined using the formula g = 4د€آ²l / Tآ². What is the uncertainty in the calculated value for g?

A student carried out an experiment in which an electric current was known to decrease with time. The readings he found, from first to last, were 3.62 mA, 2.81 mA, 1.13 mA, 1.76 mA and 0.90 mA. Which statement could not explain the anomalous 1.13 mA reading?
options: He has reversed the third and fourth readings in the results table.
He read the ammeter incorrectly; the reading should have been 2.13mA.
He took the current reading at the wrong time.
There was a systematic error in the readings from the ammeter.

questions.question: Which statement is incorrect by a factor of 100 or more?
options: Atmospheric pressure is about 1 أ— 10âپµ Pa.
Light takes 5 أ— 10آ² s to reach us from the Sun.
The frequency of ultra-violet light is 3 أ— 10آ¹آ² Hz.
The life-span of a man is about 2 أ— 10âپ¹ s.

questions.question: What is the approximate temperature of a red-hot ring on an electric cooker?
Which quantity has the same base units as momentum?
What is the approximate kinetic energy of an Olympic athlete when running at maximum speed during a 100 m race?

The speed of a car is calculated from measurements of the distance travelled and the time taken. The distance is measured as 200 m, with an uncertainty of آ±2 m. The time is measured as 10.0 s, with an uncertainty of آ±0.2 s. What is the percentage uncertainty in the calculated speed?
The units of all physical quantities can be expressed in terms of SI base units. Which pair contains quantities with the same base units?
A 1.5V cell supplies 0.20 A to a lamp for seven hours before the lamp goes out. What is a sensible estimate for the initial chemical energy content of the cell?
Three of these quantities have the same unit. Which quantity has a different unit?

In an experiment to determine the acceleration of free fall g, the time t taken for a ball to fall through distance s was measured. The uncertainty in the measurement of s is estimated to be 2%. The uncertainty in the measurement of t is estimated to be 3%. The value of g is determined using the equation g = 2s / tآ². What is the uncertainty in the calculated value of g?
What is a possible unit for the product VI, where V is the potential difference across a resistor and I is the current through the same resistor?
options: newton metre per second (N m sâپ»آ¹)

questions.question: What is a reasonable estimate of the average kinetic energy of an athlete during a 100 m race that takes 10s?
In an experiment to determine the acceleration of free fall using a falling body, what would lead to a value that is too large?
options: dimensions of the body are too large
measured distance longer than true distance
measured time longer than true time

questions.question: For which quantity is the magnitude a reasonable estimate?
options: frequency of a radio wave: 500 pHz
the Young modulus of a metal: 500 kPa

questions.question: An Olympic athlete of mass 80 kg competes in a 100 m race. What is the best estimate of his mean kinetic energy during the race?
What is the ratio (10âپ»آ³ THz) / (10آ³ kHz)?

The following physical quantities can be either positive or negative: s (displacement along a straight line), خ¸ (temperature on the Celsius scale), q (electric charge), V (readings on a digital voltmeter). Which of these quantities are vectors?

A micrometer is used to measure the diameters of two cylinders. Diameter of first cylinder = 12.78 آ± 0.02 mm, diameter of second cylinder = 16.24 آ± 0.03 mm. The difference in the diameters is calculated. What is the uncertainty in this difference?
To check calculations, the units are put into the following equations together with the numbers. Which equation must be incorrect?
In making reasonable estimates of physical quantities, which statement is not correct?
options: The frequency of sound can be of the order of GHz.
The wavelength of light can be of the order of 600 nm.
The Young modulus can be of the order of 10آ¹آ¹ Pa.
Beta radiation is associated with one unit of negative charge.

questions.question: A metal sphere of radius r is dropped into a tank of water. As it sinks at speed v, it experiences a drag force F given by F = krv, where k is a constant. What are the SI base units of k?

The frictional force F on a sphere falling through a fluid is given by the formula F = 6د€خ·av where a is the radius of the sphere, خ· is a constant relating to the fluid and v is the velocity of the sphere. What are the units of خ·?
The SI unit for potential difference (the volt) is given, in base units, by
The product of pressure and volume has the same SI base units as
An ion is accelerated by a series of electrodes in a vacuum. A graph of the power supplied to the ion is plotted against time. What is represented by the area under the graph between two times?
options: the change in kinetic energy of the ion
the average force on the ion
the change in momentum of the ion
the change in velocity of the ion

questions.question: The stress needed to fracture a particular solid is given by the equation دƒ = k * sqrt(خ³E / d) where E is the Young modulus, d is the distance between planes of atoms, and k is a constant with no units. What are the SI base units of خ³?

At temperatures close to 0K, the specific heat capacity c of a particular solid is given by c = bTآ³, where T is the thermodynamic temperature and b is a constant characteristic of the solid. The SI unit of specific heat capacity is J kgâپ»آ¹ Kâپ»آ¹. What is the unit of constant b, expressed in SI base units?

A student measures the time T for one complete oscillation of a pendulum of length l. Her results are: l = 0.420 آ± 0.001 m, T = 1.3 آ± 0.1 s. She uses the formula T = 2د€ * sqrt(l/g) to calculate the acceleration of free fall g. What is the best estimate of the percentage uncertainty in the value of g?

When a constant braking force is applied to a vehicle moving at speed v, the distance d moved by the vehicle in coming to rest is given by the expression d = kvآ² where k is a constant. When d is measured in metres and v is measured in metres per second, the constant has a value of kâ‚پ. What is the value of the constant when the distance is measured in metres, and the speed is measured in kilometres per hour?

The strain energy W of a spring is determined from its spring constant k and extension x. The spring obeys Hooke's law and the value of W is calculated using W = 0.5kxآ². The spring constant is 100 آ± 2 N mâپ»آ¹ and the extension is 0.050 آ± 0.002 m. What is the percentage uncertainty in the calculated value of W?

In a simple electrical circuit, the current in a resistor is measured as (2.50 آ± 0.05) mA. The resistor is marked as having a value of 4.7 خ© آ± 2%. If these values were used to calculate the power dissipated in the resistor, what would be the percentage uncertainty in the value obtained?
What is equivalent to the unit of electric field strength?

A steel wire is stretched in an experiment to determine the Young modulus for steel. The uncertainties in the measurements are given below: load on wire آ±2%, length of wire آ±0.2%, diameter of wire آ±1.5%, extension آ±1%. What is the percentage uncertainty in the Young modulus?

The resistance R of a resistor is to be determined. The current I in the resistor and the potential difference V across it are measured. The results, with their uncertainties, are I = (2.0 آ± 0.2) A and V = (15.0 آ± 0.5) V. The value of R is calculated to be 7.5 خ©. What is the uncertainty in this value for R?

An experiment is carried out to measure the resistance of a wire. The current in the wire is (1.0 آ± 0.2) A and the potential difference across the wire is (8.0 آ± 0.4) V. What is the resistance of the wire and its uncertainty?

The Young modulus of the material of a wire is to be found. The Young modulus E is given by the equation E = 4Fl / (د€dآ²x). The wire is extended by a known force and the following measurements are made: length of wire l = 2.043 آ± 0.002 m, diameter of wire d = 0.54 آ± 0.02 mm, force applied F = 19.62 آ± 0.01 N, extension of wire x = 5.2 آ± 0.2 mm. Which measurement has the largest effect on the uncertainty in the value of the calculated Young modulus?
options: length of wire before force applied (l)
extension of wire with force applied (x)

questions.question: A thermometer can be read to an accuracy of آ±0.5 آ°C. This thermometer is used to measure a temperature rise from 40 آ°C to 100 آ°C. What is the percentage uncertainty in the measurement of the temperature rise?

A micrometer screw gauge is used to measure the diameter of a small uniform steel sphere. The micrometer reading is 5.00 mm آ± 0.01 mm. What will be the percentage uncertainty in a calculation of the volume of the sphere, using these values?

The spring constant k of a coiled wire spring is given by the equation k = Grâپ´ / (4nRآ³) where r is the radius of the wire, n is the number of turns of wire and R is the radius of each of the turns of wire. The quantity G depends on the material from which the wire is made. What is a suitable unit for G?
The unit of resistivity, expressed in terms of base units, is given by kg xآ³ yâپ»آ² zâپ»آ³. Which base units are x, y and z?
options: x = ampere, y = metre, z = second
x = metre, y = ampere, z = second
x = metre, y = second, z = ampere
x = second, y = ampere, z = metre

questions.question: In an experiment, a radio-controlled car takes 2.50 آ± 0.05 s to travel 40.0 آ± 0.1 m. What is the car's average speed and the uncertainty in this value?

The Young modulus of the material of a wire is to be found. The Young modulus E is given by the equation E = 4Fl / (د€dآ²x). The wire is extended by a known force and the following measurements are made: length of wire l = 2.043 آ± 0.002 m, diameter of wire d = 0.54 آ± 0.02 mm, force applied F = 19.62 آ± 0.01 N, extension of wire x = 5.2 آ± 0.2 mm. Which measurement has the largest effect on the uncertainty in the value of the calculated Young modulus?
options: length of wire before force applied (l)
extension of wire with force applied (x)

questions.question: A quantity x is to be determined from the equation x = P - Q. P is measured as 1.27 آ± 0.02 m. Q is measured as 0.83 آ± 0.01 m. What is the percentage uncertainty in x to one significant figure?

A student finds the density of a liquid by measuring its mass and its volume. The following is a summary of his measurements: mass of empty beaker = (20 آ± 1) g, mass of beaker + liquid = (70 آ± 1) g, volume of liquid = (10.0 آ± 0.6) cmآ³. He correctly calculates the density of the liquid as 5.0 g cmâپ»آ³. What is the uncertainty in this value?

Two cars X and Y are travelling in the same direction. X is 50 m behind Y and has a constant velocity of 30 msâپ»آ¹. Y has a constant velocity of 20 msâپ»آ¹. What is the value of time t when X is level with Y?
A ball travels from point X to point Y around a semi-circular track of radius 1.0 m. What is the magnitude and direction of the displacement of the ball from X to Y?
A body falling in a uniform gravitational field encounters air resistance. The air resistance increases until terminal velocity is reached. Which factor does not affect its terminal velocity?
options: the height from which the body falls

questions.question: An experiment is performed to measure the acceleration of free fall g. A body falls between two fixed points. Which measurement is not required for the calculation of g?
options: the distance fallen by the body
the initial velocity of the body
the time taken for the body to fall

questions.question: A ball is thrown vertically in air. Neglecting air resistance, which property of the ball can never be zero at any time during the flight?
Which feature of a graph allows acceleration to be determined?
options: the area under a displacement-time graph
the area under a velocity-time graph
the slope of a displacement-time graph
the slope of a velocity-time graph

questions.question: A body falling in a uniform gravitational field encounters air resistance. The air resistance increases until terminal velocity is reached. Which factor does not affect its terminal velocity?
options: the height from which the body falls

questions.question: A boy throws a ball vertically upwards. It rises to a maximum height, where it is momentarily at rest, and then falls back to his hands. Which row gives the acceleration of the ball at various stages in its motion? (Take vertically upwards as positive. Ignore air resistance.)
options: rising = -9.81 msâپ»آ², at maximum height = 0, falling = +9.81 msâپ»آ²
rising = -9.81 msâپ»آ², at maximum height = -9.81 msâپ»آ², falling = -9.81 msâپ»آ²
rising = +9.81 msâپ»آ², at maximum height = +9.81 msâپ»آ², falling = +9.81 msâپ»آ²
rising = +9.81 msâپ»آ², at maximum height = 0, falling = -9.81 msâپ»آ²

questions.question: An insect jumps with an initial vertical velocity of 1.0 msâپ»آ¹, reaching a maximum height of 3.5 أ— 10âپ»آ² m. Assume the deceleration is uniform. What is the magnitude of the deceleration?
A body having uniform acceleration a increases its velocity from u to v in time t. Which expression would not give a correct value for the body's displacement during time t?

A sprinter runs a 100 m race in a straight line. He accelerates from the starting block at a constant acceleration of 2.5 msâپ»آ² to reach his maximum speed of 10 msâپ»آ¹. He maintains this speed until he crosses the finish line. Which time does it take the sprinter to run the race?

The acceleration of free fall on the Moon is one-sixth of that on Earth. On Earth it takes time t for a stone to fall from rest a distance of 2 m. What is the time taken for a stone to fall from rest a distance of 2 m on the Moon?

A tennis ball is thrown horizontally in air from the top of a tall building. If the effect of air resistance is not negligible, what happens to the horizontal and vertical components of the ball's velocity?
options: horizontal = constant, vertical = increases at a constant rate
horizontal = decreases to zero, vertical = increases at a constant rate
horizontal = decreases to zero, vertical = increases to a maximum value

questions.question: An object is thrown with velocity 5.2 msâپ»آ¹ vertically upwards on the Moon. The acceleration due to gravity on the Moon is 1.62 msâپ»آ². What is the time taken for the object to return to its starting point?

In a cathode-ray tube, an electron is accelerated uniformly in a straight line from a speed of 4 أ— 10آ³ msâپ»آ¹ to 2 أ— 10âپ· msâپ»آ¹ over a distance of 10 mm. What is the acceleration of the electron?

On a particular railway, a train driver applies the brake of the train at a yellow signal, a distance of 1.0 km from a red signal, where the train stops. The maximum deceleration of the train is 0.20 msâپ»آ². Assuming uniform deceleration, what is the maximum safe speed of the train at the yellow signal?

A person, travelling on a motorway a total distance of 200 km, travels the first 90 km at an average speed of 80 kmhâپ»آ¹. Which average speed must be obtained for the rest of the journey if the person is to reach the destination in a total time of 2 hours 0 minutes?
A cannon fires a cannonball with an initial speed v at an angle خ± to the horizontal. Which equation is correct for the maximum height H reached?
A body is released from rest and falls vertically in air of constant density. Which statement about the motion of the falling body is correct?
options: As it accelerates, its weight decreases so that its acceleration decreases until it travels with constant velocity.
It accelerates initially at 9.8 msâپ»آ² but the drag force increases so its acceleration decreases.
Its velocity increases at a constant rate until its velocity becomes constant.
The drag force of the air increases continually and eventually the velocity decreases.

questions.question: A goods train passes through a station at a steady speed of 10 msâپ»آ¹. An express train is at rest at the station. The express train leaves the station with a uniform acceleration of 0.5 msâپ»آ² just as the goods train goes past. Both trains move in the same direction on straight, parallel tracks. How much time passes before the express train overtakes the goods train?
A science museum designs an experiment to show the fall of a feather in a vertical glass vacuum tube. The time of fall from rest is to be close to 0.5 s. What length of tube is required?

A bicycle brakes so that it undergoes uniform deceleration from a speed of 8 msâپ»آ¹ to 6 msâپ»آ¹ over a distance of 7 m. If the deceleration of the bicycle remains constant, what further distance will it travel before coming to rest?
A projectile is launched at point O and follows a parabolic path. Air resistance may be neglected. Which statement is true for the projectile when it is at the highest point of its path?
options: The horizontal component of the projectile's acceleration is zero.
The horizontal component of the projectile's velocity is zero.
The kinetic energy of the projectile is zero.
The momentum of the projectile is zero.

questions.question: A boy throws a ball vertically upwards. It rises to a maximum height, where it is momentarily at rest, and then falls back to his hands. Which row gives the acceleration of the ball at various stages in its motion? (Take vertically upwards as positive. Ignore air resistance.)
options: rising = -9.81 msâپ»آ², at maximum height = 0, falling = +9.81 msâپ»آ²
rising = -9.81 msâپ»آ², at maximum height = -9.81 msâپ»آ², falling = -9.81 msâپ»آ²
rising = +9.81 msâپ»آ², at maximum height = +9.81 msâپ»آ², falling = +9.81 msâپ»آ²
rising = +9.81 msâپ»آ², at maximum height = 0, falling = -9.81 msâپ»آ²

questions.question: A stone of mass m is dropped from a tall building. There is significant air resistance. The acceleration of free fall is g. When the stone reaches its terminal velocity, which information is correct?
options: acceleration = g, gravity force = mg, air resistance force = mg
acceleration = zero, gravity force = mg, air resistance force = mg
acceleration = zero, gravity force = zero, air resistance force = mg
acceleration = zero, gravity force = zero, air resistance force = zero

questions.question: The water surface in a deep well is 78.0m below the top of the well. A person at the top of the well drops a heavy stone down the well. Air resistance is negligible. The speed of sound in the air is 330 msâپ»آ¹. What is the time interval between the person dropping the stone and hearing it hitting the water?

In an experiment to determine the acceleration of free fall g, a ball bearing is held by an electromagnet. When the current to the electromagnet is switched off, a clock starts and the ball bearing falls. After falling a distance h, the ball bearing strikes a switch to stop the clock which measures the time t of the fall. If systematic errors cause t and h to be measured incorrectly, which error must cause g to appear greater than 9.81 msâپ»آ²?
options: h measured as being smaller than it actually is and t is measured correctly
h measured as being smaller than it actually is and t measured as being larger than it actually is
h measured as being larger than it actually is and t measured as being larger than it actually is
h is measured correctly and t measured as being smaller than it actually is

questions.question: A firework rocket is fired vertically upwards. The fuel burns and produces a constant upwards force on the rocket. After 5 seconds there is no fuel left. Air resistance is negligible. What is the acceleration before and after 5 seconds?
